key_insight: Restricting networks to 3D representations lets the authors compare architectures by how strongly they can change low-dimensional topology, especially linking number.
engineering_relevance: The results suggest that architectural choices like skip connections, attention, and activation shape can directly affect expressivity in ways that topology can help quantify.
method: Model layers in ^3 to isolate depth/activation effects from width.
Track changes in linking number across feedforward nets, ResNets, transformers, and flow-based models.
Prove expressivity comparisons and validate them with experiments.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports full proofs plus empirical experiments supporting the expressivity hierarchy across architecture classes.
watch_item: The conclusions are derived from a very low-dimensional setting, so translating the topology-based ranking to practical high-width models may require care.
paper_title: Low-dimensional topology of deep neural networks
